signals.title: NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 95.21 (implied) — NZD outperformance dominates G8 space with a 2.212% spread over laggard JPY. Despite NZD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading strength), the pair is supported by a +152bp OIS carry advantage (RBNZ 2.25% vs BoJ 0.73%). US-Iran deal optimism and record US equity highs (S&P 500 7,578) sustain risk-on appetite, keeping JPY carry-unwind pressure at bay while VIX remains at 15.3 (normal volatility).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5985 — COT LF net short -15,435 (28% long) vs RBNZ hike bias (next CB meeting). Retail sentiment is at an 86% short extreme, diverging from the hawkish CB bias and institutional positioning. This creates a high-conviction contrarian setup; retail capitulation and spec short-unwinding drive the 1.81% weekly gain. Watch for a break above 0.6000 to accelerate the squeeze.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7186 — COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) represents a crowded institutional long. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with IV at 16.0% vs HV30 8.4% (VRP +7.6%), while 25d RR at -0.70 signals a persistent put skew. Despite the -60bp carry deficit vs USD (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), AUD strength persists on risk-on sentiment. Crowded positioning and high vol premium suggest late-trend exhaustion risk.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7820 — Retail sentiment at 86% long extreme vs COT LF net short -6,015. Retail is aggressively fading the 0.57% weekly decline. With a +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%), the pair remains structurally bid, but retail positioning suggests a contrarian risk of further downside if USD funding stress (DXY vs SPX corr z=+1.71σ) continues to ease.
